citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
-A female teddy/mix tag #3256 had a physical exam on 3/16/2026 and an eye ulcer noted on her right eye and treatment
instructions from the Attending Veterinarian (AV) to apply triple antibiotic ophthalmic ointment. The licensee said that the
AV left the ophthalmic ointment that day and the eye needed to be treated, however has not been applying the treatment.
The affected eye has a small area of cloudiness with small veins in the middle of the cloudy area which can be a sign that
it has not fully healed. Not starting treatment for an eye condition per the attending veterinarian's treatments could cause
discomfort in an eye and could cause it to get worse.
-A male red poodle tag #3228 had a physical exam on 3/16/2026 and noted that ears are dirty and inflamed and need to
be cleaned with the ear cleaner solution at the kennel. The licensee said that the ears have not yet been cleaned as
instructed. The ears observed today had a dark material in both and a slight bad odor to them. Not following the attending
veterinarian's instructions on ear treatments can cause discomfort and the problem might not improve without treatment.
Ensure to give treatments as instructed by the attending veterinarian and to communicate with the AV if there are
questions on treatment instructions.

citations.desc: Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control.
citations.narrative:
Various pieces of construction trash (insulation, tin) were scattered under the building and when grass and plants start
growing can create areas for rodent nesting and make it difficult to cut the grass/weeds. Rodent nesting areas can
increase risk of disease for animals. To protect the health of the animals, the premises must be kept free of accumulations
of trash, junk, waste products, and discarded matter.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class A license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by 5/27/2026 or applicant will forfeit the license fee and must
wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
-A female teddy/mix tag #3256 had a physical exam on 3/16/2026 and an eye ulcer noted on her right eye and treatment 
instructions from the Attending Veterinarian (AV) to apply triple antibiotic ophthalmic ointment. The licensee said that the 
AV left the ophthalmic ointment that day and the eye needed to be treated, however has not been applying the treatment. 
The affected eye has a small area of cloudiness with small veins in the middle of the cloudy area which can be a sign that 
it has not fully healed. Not starting treatment for an eye condition per the attending veterinarian's treatments could cause 
discomfort in an eye and could cause it to get worse.  
 
-A male red poodle tag #3228 had a physical exam on 3/16/2026 and noted that ears are dirty and inflamed and need to 
be cleaned with the ear cleaner solution at the kennel. The licensee said that the ears have not yet been cleaned as 
instructed. The ears observed today had a dark material in both and a slight bad odor to them. Not following the attending 
veterinarian's instructions on ear treatments can cause discomfort and the problem might not improve without treatment.  
 
Ensure to give treatments as instructed by the attending veterinarian and to communicate with the AV if there are 
questions on treatment instructions. 
3.11(c) 
   
Cleaning, sanitization, housekeeping, and pest control. 
Various pieces of construction trash (insulation, tin) were scattered under the building and when grass and plants start 
growing can create areas for rodent nesting and make it difficult to cut the grass/weeds. Rodent nesting areas can 
increase risk of disease for animals. To protect the health of the animals, the premises must be kept free of accumulations 
of trash, junk, waste products, and discarded matter. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class A license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspections or by 5/27/2026 or applicant will forfeit the license fee and must 
wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your next Re-License Inspection.

Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires. 
 
 n
